dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses sign of latest log return as directional signal. Entry: go long if log_returns > 0, go short if log_returns < 0. Exit: reverse position when sign changes (log_returns crosses zero). No explicit threshold magnitude provided in source, so threshold is set to 0 (sign change). Hold period is 1 day as signal is evaluated on most recent return only.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses sign of latest log return as directional signal. Entry: go long if log_returns > 0, go short if log_returns < 0. Exit: reverse position when sign changes (log_returns crosses zero). No explicit threshold magnitude provided in source, so threshold is set to 0 (sign change). Hold period is 1 day as signal is evaluated on most recent return only.

source_rule_text: A trader should take a long position when the most recent return is positive and a short position when it is negative, using the sign of the latest log return as the directional signal.
